title: lilium bulbiferum subsp. thunbergianum
intro: Lilium bulbiferum subsp. thunbergianum, also known as the Orange Lily, is a plant species within the lily family (Liliaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Japan and Korea. The Orange Lily has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the Orange Lily can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to skin conditions and digestive issues.
